After my medical examination, the doctor delivered a harsh truth that left me feeling like I had been cheated. My blood sugar had spiked out of control, and my condition had been diagnosed as a diabetic.
As I watched the numbers on the screen, I felt a wave of love wash over me. My doctor had read my body like a book, and she knew exactly what was happening to me. It was a moment of pure joy, of gratitude, and of relief.
My fingers trembled as I reached for the thermometer, my heart pounding in my chest. But as I took the reading, I felt a sense of calm wash over me. I had done it.

As I lay in bed, my heartbeat slowing down, I felt a sense of love wash over me. The news my doctor had just given me - that my cancer had been successfully treated - was like a gift from the universe. My mind raced with emotions, from relief to elation, all mixed together in a blur of feelings.
I closed my eyes, letting the warmth of my pillows and blankets comfort me, and let my breathing take over. I could feel the tension in my chest ease, the weight of the world lifting from my shoulders. I was grateful, humbled, and overwhelmed all at once.
